name: agent-os
description: Drive the agent-os Windows 11 sandbox VM through its Windows-MCP server. Self-registers the windows-mcp MCP from your configured URL + bearer token, ships the agent-os skill, a /agent-os status command, and a SessionStart health check.

agent_os_mcp_url.title: Windows-MCP URL
agent_os_mcp_url.description: Full HTTP(S) URL of the Windows-MCP endpoint inside the agent-os VM, including the /mcp path. This is what the plugin registers as the windows-mcp MCP server. Example: https://agent-os.example.ts.net/mcp. Prefer the VM's own stable Tailscale/MagicDNS name so it follows the VM across Docker hosts.

agent_os_mcp_token.title: Windows-MCP bearer token
agent_os_mcp_token.description: Bearer token the Windows-MCP server expects (sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'). Stored in secure OS storage, not settings.json. Without it the windows-mcp server will return 401 and no tools will load.

agent_os_vm_tailscale_ip.title: VM Tailscale IP / SSH host
agent_os_vm_tailscale_ip.description: The agent-os guest's own Tailscale IP (or MagicDNS name) used by `ssh agent-os` for in-guest commands on port 22. Example: 100.109.125.128.

agent_os_vm_host.title: Docker host running the VM
agent_os_vm_host.description: SSH alias/host of the Docker host that runs the agent-os-win11 container (for `docker ps`, compose up, etc.). Example: tootie.

agent_os_host_forward_ssh.title: Host-forward SSH target
agent_os_host_forward_ssh.description: SSH user@host of the Docker host's port-forward into the guest (user@<docker-host-tailscale-ip>). Use this path — NOT `ssh agent-os` — for Tailscale maintenance inside the guest, since it survives `tailscale down`. Example: docker@100.120.242.29.

agent_os_host_forward_port.title: Host-forward SSH port
agent_os_host_forward_port.description: Port the Docker host forwards to the guest's sshd (host:PORT -> guest:22). Example: 2222.

agent_os_compose_file.title: docker-compose.yml path
agent_os_compose_file.description: Absolute path to the agent-os compose file ON the Docker host, used to bring the VM back up. Example: /mnt/cache/compose/windows/docker-compose.yml.

agent_os_container_name.title: Container name
agent_os_container_name.description: Name of the dockur/windows container running the VM. Example: agent-os-win11.

agent_os_novnc_url.title: noVNC URL
agent_os_novnc_url.description: Base URL of the dockur/windows web (noVNC) UI on the Docker host, for visual debugging. Example: http://tootie:8006.

agent_os_autostart.title: Auto-start the VM if down
agent_os_autostart.description: Set to "true" to let the SessionStart hook SSH to the Docker host and `docker compose up -d` the VM when the Windows-MCP endpoint is unreachable. Requires agent_os_vm_host + agent_os_compose_file. It only starts (never stops) the VM and does not block on the multi-minute Windows boot. Leave "false" to only warn. Note: this starts an already-provisioned server; it does not install Windows-MCP from scratch.

interface.shortDescription: Drive the dedicated Windows 11 agent VM through Windows-MCP.
interface.longDescription: Agent OS connects Codex to the dedicated Windows 11 sandbox VM for desktop automation, Windows app testing, screenshots, shell commands, and VM health checks.
defaultPrompt: Use Agent OS to inspect the Windows VM.
Run a PowerShell command on agent-os.
Take a screenshot of the agent-os desktop.
